children.heading: Alaska Native
children.label: 1 Alaska Native
children.text: The term “Alaska Native” has the meaning given the term “Native” in .section 1602 of title 43

children.heading: Dating violence
children.label: 2 Dating violence
children.text: The term “dating violence” has the meaning given such term in .section 12291(a) of this title

children.heading: Domestic violence
children.label: 3 Domestic violence
children.text: The term “domestic violence” has the meaning given such term in .section 12291(a) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: results or threatens to result in physical injury; and

children.label: i
children.text: is related by blood;

children.label: ii
children.text: is or was related by marriage or is or was otherwise legally related; or

children.label: iii
children.text: is or was lawfully residing.

children.heading: Indian; Indian tribe; tribal organization
children.label: 5 Indian; Indian tribe; tribal organization
children.text: The terms “Indian”, “Indian tribe”, and “tribal organization” have the meanings given such terms in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: Native Hawaiian
children.label: 6 Native Hawaiian
children.text: The term “Native Hawaiian” has the meaning given the term in .section 7517 of title 20

children.heading: Personally identifying information
children.label: 7 Personally identifying information
children.text: The term “personally identifying information” has the meaning given the term in .section 12291(a) of this title

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 8 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of Health and Human Services.

children.heading: Shelter
children.label: 9 Shelter
children.text: The term “shelter” means the provision of temporary refuge and supportive services in compliance with applicable State law (including regulation) governing the provision, on a regular basis, of shelter, safe homes, meals, and supportive services to victims of family violence, domestic violence, or dating violence, and their dependents.

children.heading: State
children.label: 10 State
children.text: The term “State” means each of the several States, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and, except as otherwise provided, Guam, American Samoa, the United States Virgin Islands, and the Commonwealth of the Northern Mariana Islands.

children.label: A
children.text: has a membership that includes a majority of the primary-purpose domestic violence service providers in the State;

children.label: B
children.text: has board membership that is representative of primary-purpose domestic violence service providers, and which may include representatives of the communities in which the services are being provided in the State;

children.label: C
children.text: has as its purpose to provide education, support, and technical assistance to such service providers to enable the providers to establish and maintain shelter and supportive services for victims of domestic violence and their dependents; and

children.label: D
children.text: serves as an information clearinghouse, primary point of contact, and resource center on domestic violence for the State and supports the development of polices, protocols, and procedures to enhance domestic violence intervention and prevention in the State.

children.label: A
children.text: meet the needs of such victims of family violence, domestic violence, or dating violence, and their dependents, for short-term, transitional, or long-term safety; and

children.label: B
children.text: provide counseling, advocacy, or assistance for victims of family violence, domestic violence, or dating violence, and their dependents.

children.heading: Tribally designated official
children.label: 13 Tribally designated official
children.text: The term “tribally designated official” means an individual designated by an Indian tribe, tribal organization, or nonprofit private organization authorized by an Indian tribe, to administer a grant under .section 10409 of this title

children.heading: Underserved populations
children.label: 14 Underserved populations
children.text: The term “underserved populations” has the meaning given the term in . For the purposes of this chapter, the Secretary has the same authority to determine whether a population is an underserved population as the Attorney General has under that section 12291(a) of this title.section 12291(a) of this title1So in original.1
